summary: Global markets are broadly risk-on following a preliminary US-Iran peace deal that has halted hostilities and signaled a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, lifting European equities with the STOXX 600 hitting a record high while oil prices tumbled on the prospect of normalized Persian Gulf shipping lanes. The US-Iran agreement, discussed on the sidelines of the G7 summit in France, represents the dominant macro catalyst of the moment, easing geopolitical risk premiums that had been embedded across energy, shipping, and safe-haven assets. In notable corporate developments, Fox announced a $22 billion acquisition of Roku, Salesforce is advancing its AI platform positioning, and investors are adding exposure to consumer banking names that benefit from lower oil-driven inflation pressures. Despite the diplomatic breakthrough, key risks remain firmly in focus, including lingering uncertainty over Hormuz mine clearance and transit safety, a weakening yen that the Iran deal appears unlikely to rescue, and the Bank of Japan's unaltered rate-hike trajectory which continues to pressure carry trades. In the short term, the path of least resistance favors bulls as oil deflation supports consumer spending and margins, though investors will closely watch the durability of the Iran ceasefire, Fed policy signals, and whether Hormuz shipping volumes actually normalize by August as prediction markets currently suggest.
